items.title: Medical diagnosis and screening
items.description: Bayes' theorem converts test accuracy (sensitivity, specificity) and disease prevalence into P(disease | positive test)—essential for interpreting screening results without over-treating false positives.

items.title: Spam and abuse classification
items.description: Email spam filters, fraud detectors, and LLM safety classifiers use Bayes-style updates: combine class priors with feature likelihoods to rank P(class | observation).

items.title: Naive Bayes text classification
items.description: Document classifiers estimate P(category | word counts) via Bayes' theorem with a conditional-independence assumption, giving fast, interpretable baselines for sentiment and topic labeling.

items.title: A/B test and experiment analysis
items.description: Bayesian A/B testing treats variant performance as hypotheses updated with conversion data; Bayes' rule yields posterior probabilities that a variant beats the control.

items.title: Anomaly and incident triage
items.description: On-call systems combine low base rates of real incidents with alert precision to estimate P(true incident | alert), prioritizing human review for high-posterior cases.
